*A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

DAVID J. GARIBALDI, III
OF COUNSEL

November 6, 1990


Ms. Louisa Menchaca
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

     Re: FPPC Regulations Concerning Newsletters

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

     This letter is to confirm our conversation today in which I sought your advice concerning a mass mailing issue. As I explained the situation, the members of a city council gave editorial input into the contents of a city newsletter. The names of the councilmembers themselves do not appear in the newsletter, but the city council as a body is referenced. I was concerned of a possible violation of FPPC Regulation 18901.

     After checking reference sources, you stated that you believe that a newsletter which is prepared in consultation with the city council may mention the city council as a body, so long as no individual offices or names appear in the newsletter. You stated that you believed that the FPPC had previously issued opinions to this effect. You also advised me as to the availability of seeking a formal opinion letter for this particular situation.

     Thank you for your help in this matter. If you have any questions, or believe that this letter does not reflect the

RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

Ms. Louisa Menchaca
November 6, 1990
Page 2


substance of our conversation, please give me a call.

Sincerely yours,

RUTAN & TUCKER

[signature]

Robert Owen

RO:jl
